title: S5: bee knowledge context --work --budget — the budget-aware consumer, dogfooded on real data
action: Per D27, extend knowledge.mjs (templates/lib SOURCE OF TRUTH, same atomic propagation as okf-1/okf-4) with `context`: resolve --work <id> to the bee.work-item concept whose bee.id matches; walk its bee.required_context TRANSITIVELY (each visited concept's own required_context too) with a cycle guard — a cycle is deduped silently, never an error; add every bundle concept of type bee.decision whose bee.areas overlaps the work item's areas (informational: also surface the work item's bee.decisions id list in the output header); add EVERY concept with bee.critical true; rank: (1) the work item itself, (2) its bee.plan sibling in the same work/<id>/ dir when present, (3) required_context in BFS depth order, (4) critical patterns, (5) area decisions; cut at --budget <tokens> using bytes/4 (estimator NAMED in output as bytes/4); OUTPUT = ordered MANIFEST — one entry per concept: path, bytes, est_tokens, one-line reason ('work item', 'required_context depth 1 via <parent>', 'critical pattern', ...) — NEVER file content. --json emits {work,decisions,budget,estimator,total_est,entries:[...],truncated:[paths]}; human form is a table. Unknown --work id = typed error exit 1. Register knowledge.context in command-registry (runnable example: --work okf-foundation --budget 20000); wire HANDLERS. RED-FIRST: extend test_knowledge.mjs — fixture bundle with a work item, 2-level required_context chain, a cycle (A->B->A), a critical pattern, a non-critical pattern (excluded), an area decision, budget large (all in, order asserted), budget tiny (cut asserted, truncated list named), unknown id error, manifest never contains body text. Then the atomic propagation sequence (onboard --apply, render trees, manifest --write) as LAST mutations; ONE commit sweeping everything.

truths: node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0 (65 suites; test_knowledge extended in place)
red evidence: new tests failed before implementation (verbatim)

DOGFOOD (S5 acceptance, integration review): `node .bee/bin/bee.mjs knowledge context --work okf-foundation --budget 20000 --json` returns a manifest whose entries all exist on disk, headed by work-item.md and plan.md, including areas/advisor-protocol via required_context and every critical pattern that fits; total_est <= 20000; full output quoted in the trace
manifest entries carry path/bytes/est_tokens/reason and never content
cycle fixture dedupes silently; unknown --work exits 1 with a typed error
prohibitions: never emit file content in the manifest (D27)
no tokenizer dependency — bytes/4 only, named (D12/D27)
no reads outside docs/knowledge/ except resolving nothing — the verb is bundle-only (D23); the decisions id list comes from the work item's own frontmatter, never from .bee/ stores

trace.outcome: bee knowledge context --work --budget shipped (D27): transitive required_context walk with silent cycle dedupe, critical + area-decision tiers, bytes/4 budget cut, ordered manifest of paths/sizes/reasons, never content — dogfooded on the real bundle (49 entries, 19781/20000 est tokens)

{
  "tests_inspected": [
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_knowledge.mjs (36 pre-existing checks over check/index/list)",
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs (registry-example conformance, 237 pre-existing checks)"
  ],
  "tests_added_or_changed": [
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_knowledge.mjs — 7 new checks: full ranked manifest + cycle dedupe, entry shape/bytes/est_tokens/no-content, per-tier reason strings incl. depth+parent, --budget prefix cut + truncated list + zero budget, unknown --work typed error (json + stderr), lonely work item (no plan/no decisions/dangling link), knowledge.context in the --help --json manifest",
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs — 1 new check: the knowledge.context registry example (--work okf-foundation --budget 20000 --json) executes green against a seeded isolated fixture bundle, paths exist, no content"
  ],
  "red_failure_evidence": "BEFORE the implementation, with the same tests in place, `node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_knowledge.mjs` printed `36 passed, 7 failed` and the dispatcher's own prior behavior for this surface was verbatim: FAIL knowledge context: an unknown --work id exits 1 with a typed error (D27) — Error: the failure must carry the typed unknown_work code, got {\"error\":\"Unknown command \\\"context\\\". Use: check, index, list.\"} ; plus FAIL 'CLI: knowledge context --json on a large budget returns the full ranked manifest in D27 order' (JSON.parse of the empty stdout threw), FAIL 'manifest entries carry path/bytes/est_tokens/reason', FAIL 'reasons name the tier ... (TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading find))', FAIL 'cuts at --budget (TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading length))', FAIL 'a bundle with no plan sibling ... got 1', FAIL 'knowledge.context appears in the bee --help --json manifest — Error: manifest must carry knowledge.context'. test_bee_cli.mjs was simultaneously `237 passed, 1 failed` on the new registry-example check. So the prior behavior was: `bee knowledge context` did not exist — the group fallback rejected the verb with `Unknown command \"context\". Use: check, index, list.` and the --help manifest carried no knowledge.context entry.",
  "verification_run": "node scripts/run_verify.mjs -> PASS run_verify: 65 suite(s), concurrency=5, wall=58020ms (0 failures). test_knowledge.mjs 43 passed / 0 failed (was 36/7 red); test_bee_cli.mjs 238 passed / 0 failed (was 237/1 red).",
  "dogfood_acceptance": "node .bee/bin/bee.mjs knowledge context --work okf-foundation --budget 20000 --json -> 49 entries, truncated 1, total_est 19781 <= budget 20000, estimator \"bytes/4\". Order verified against the D27 ranking law: [1] docs/knowledge/work/okf-foundation/work-item.md (reason 'work item'), [2] docs/knowledge/work/okf-foundation/plan.md ('plan sibling in work/okf-foundation/'), [3-6] the four required_context targets at depth 1 via work-item.md (areas/advisor-protocol/overview.md + 3 patterns), [7-49] 43 critical patterns path-sorted; the 47th critical pattern (20260721-shared-file-axes-must-be-sequenced-at-dispatch.md) is the single truncated entry. Programmatic re-check: 0 entries missing on disk, 0 entries whose bytes/est_tokens disagree with fs.statSync(path).size and Math.ceil(size/4), sum(est_tokens)==total_est==19781, every truncated path exists on disk. No area decisions appear because the bundle holds no bee.decision concept yet (the tier is exercised by the fixture instead). Unknown id: `knowledge context --work nope --budget 100` -> stderr 'knowledge context: unknown_work — no bee.work-item concept in docs/knowledge/ carries bee.id \"nope\" (D27).', exit 1.",
  "prohibitions_checked": "No file content in the manifest (entries carry path/bytes/est_tokens/reason only; the fixture body marker 'Body.' and the real bundle prose never appear — asserted in both the JSON and human forms). No tokenizer: sizing is Math.ceil(bytes/4) via fs.statSync, and CONTEXT_ESTIMATOR='bytes/4' is emitted in every manifest. Bundle-only reads: buildContextManifest reads solely through collectConcepts(root)/bundleDir(root) plus fs.statSync inside docs/knowledge/, and the `decisions` header comes from the work item's own bee.decisions frontmatter, never a .bee/ store. No --strict added anywhere; docs/decisions/index.md untouched.",
  "deliberate_exceptions": [
    "The BFS over required_context is seeded with the work item AND its plan sibling (the action names the work item's own chain). Rationale: the plan is already in the manifest, so what the plan requires is required context too; every reason still names its parent, so provenance stays auditable. On the real bundle this changes nothing (plan.md's only required_context is the work item, which is already selected and therefore deduped silently)."
  ]
}
